mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate signals labor market tightening and rising wage pressures, which compress corporate profit margins and reduce equity valuations. This mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.1% as of September 2011 reflects elevated unemployment post-financial crisis
failure_condition: Rank IC of UNRATE momentum signal exceeds 0.05 in absolute value over any 2-year rolling window, or negative forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth (RSAFS) falls below its 6-month moving average while simultaneously housing starts (HOUST) decline, aggregate demand weakens. This joint deterioration signals reduced consumer spending and construction activity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 571k units in August 2011 reflects weak housing recovery
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST decline simultaneously, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the interaction signal falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at near-zero levels (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals monetary policy exhaustion and loss of policy accommodation credibility. When combined with elevated inflation expectations (CPIAUCSL rising), the central bank faces a policy bind that increases equity risk premium and produces negative forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% AND CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.3% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08% as of September 2011 reflects zero lower bound constraint
regime_hint: monetary policy constraint with inflation pressure
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction signal's Rank IC falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) serves as a real-time proxy for manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side health. A sustained month-over-month decline in INDPRO signals deteriorating production capacity and reduced output expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice near-term earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 94.04 in August 2011 reflects post-crisis production levels
failure_condition: When INDPRO declines > 0.5% month-over-month, S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances, or Rank IC of the signal falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) reflects labor market momentum and consumer income generation capacity. When PAYEMS growth turns negative or stalls (month-over-month change near zero), it signals imminent demand destruction and reduced household purchasing power, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as forward earnings are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below 0.05% (near-zero or negative growth)
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 131,334k in September 2011 reflects slow post-crisis employment recovery
failure_condition: When PAYEMS growth falls below 0.05% month-over-month, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly serves as the broadest demand signal. When GDPC1 growth decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate, it signals late-cycle weakness and earnings recession risk. This mechanism produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as investors reprice terminal growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 1.5% in the most recent quarter
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13,271.8B (2017 dollars) as of Q2 2011 reflects slowing growth trajectory
failure_condition: When GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5% annualized, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC of the signal falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) at near-zero levels combined with declining inflation (CPIAUCSL month-over-month change negative) signals monetary policy effectiveness and real rate accommodation. This combination produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors gain confidence in policy support and real asset valuations rise.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 0.25% AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month change is negative or flat (< 0.1%)
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08% and CPIAUCSL at 226.27 in August 2011
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC of the interaction signal falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) forms a supply-labor interaction signal. When both INDPRO and PAYEMS show month-over-month growth above their respective 3-month averages, it signals robust supply-side expansion and labor productivity gains, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as profit margin expectations improve.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth exceeds its 3-month average AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth exceeds its 3-month average
evidence_refs: fact:Both series show modest growth in 2011 post-crisis recovery
regime_hint: supply-side expansion with labor productivity gains
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC of the interaction signal falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
